column1: # Actions: PASS (ignore), WARN (log only, don't fail), FAIL (fail the scan)
#   • FAIL on alerts that represent genuine exploitable risk on this stack
#   • WARN on alerts that need review but are likely false positives in this
#     lab context (no production CSP, internal-only ZAP scan, etc.)
#   • PASS on alerts that are intentionally not addressed at this phase
# Run `docker run zaproxy/zap-stable zap.sh -alert-list` for the full list.
# ── Injections (FAIL — these are the point of the lab) ──────────────────────
# PASS in local/CI context — no TLS termination in the DAST stack
# 10038 Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
# WARN only — CSP not yet configured in this phase
# 10024 Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL
# 10025 Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in HTTP Referrer Header

column2: PASS    # Source maps / build comments in dev build

column1: # PASS — API uses JWT bearer auth (stateless), no session cookies, CSRF N/A

column2: PASS    # DAST stack intentionally uses HTTP

column1: # ── Low-signal false positives in this lab context ──────────────────────────
# 10037 X-Powered-By Header — fixed by helmet (app.disable handled internally)
# 10049 Storable and Cacheable Content — helmet sets Cache-Control: no-store
# 10063 Permissions-Policy Header Not Set — added explicitly in index.js
# 90005 Sec-Fetch-Dest Header Missing — this is a *request* header sent by browsers.
# ZAP's HTTP client doesn't send it. Not a server-side finding. Suppress entirely.
# Use IGNORE (not PASS) — baseline script treats PASS as "known baseline item"
# whereas IGNORE actively suppresses the alert regardless of baseline state.
